title: Tabulation and state reconstruction

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Tabulation and state reconstruction; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Tabulation and state reconstruction
sources.claim: Tabulation orders subproblems bottom-up so dependencies are available before each state is computed.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Tabulation orders subproblems bottom-up so dependencies are available before each state is computed.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A coin-change table initializes zero amount and fills larger amounts from previously solved smaller amounts. Your goal is to design and implement one- and two-dimensional dynamic programs with explanation of state and transition. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Recursion and divide-and-conquer; Hash tables, sets and maps; Overlapping subproblems and memoization; Two-dimensional arrays and matrices. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Tabulation orders subproblems bottom-up so dependencies are available before each state is computed. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Iteration order, base initialization, impossible states, and memory compression depend on the recurrence graph. The draft therefore rejects: Dynamic-programming table order never matters because all cells are eventually filled.

includes: Order subproblems bottom-up, optimize storage when justified and reconstruct a chosen solution.
Design and implement one- and two-dimensional dynamic programs with explanation of state and transition.

Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
excludes: Iteration order, base initialization, impossible states, and memory compression depend on the recurrence graph.

outcomes: Design and implement one- and two-dimensional dynamic programs with explanation of state and transition.

examples.title: Tabulation and state reconstruction: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: A coin-change table initializes zero amount and fills larger amounts from previously solved smaller amounts.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: A coin-change table initializes zero amount and fills larger amounts from previously solved smaller amounts.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Fill states only after their dependencies; derive loop order from the recurrence, not table appearance.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Fill states only after their dependencies; derive loop order from the recurrence, not table appearance.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Iteration order, base initialization, impossible states, and memory compression depend on the recurrence graph.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Tabulation and state reconstruction, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
